updated: 2026-04-21T16:42:38.000Z
---

# 23people Company Handbook Summary

> This document provides a synthesized overview optimized for AI consumption.
> It focuses on stable patterns, principles, and frameworks rather than
> frequently changing operational data.

## About Us

### Who We Are

23people SpA is a Chilean technology company founded on November 26, 2014, by Marcelo Ampuero and Manuel Reyes — two Software Engineers and Computer Scientists who continue to lead the company as Engineering Director and General Director respectively. The company was born from a simple but powerful idea: to create the kind of company where the founders themselves would have loved to work. 23people describes itself as a team of professionals genuinely passionate about technology, committed to building a sustainable business model that places people first — both clients and professionals — while pursuing positive participation in society.

The company's mission is to act as trusted technology facilitators and co-creators for companies seeking to evolve their digital products and services. 23people empowers and co-creates alongside teams facing the constant challenge of Digital Business Evolution, helping them integrate Tech Talent, Software Engineering, and Technologies with their business knowledge and data potential.

The name "23people" originates from the Matrix film saga — specifically from a dialogue in The Matrix Reloaded where The Architect refers to selecting "twenty-three individuals" to rebuild Zion. In summary, 23people represents "the spirit behind those 23 people outside the matrix."

The three core values shared by everyone at 23people are: **Excellence and Intellectual Curiosity** (continuous learning, iterative improvement, pursuit of technical excellence), **Collaboration and Assertive Communication** (genuine support, honest transparency, open communication channels prioritized over private ones), and **Sustainable Growth and Becoming a Reference** (ambitious economic growth at a systematic, sustainable pace — not at the expense of people or natural resources).

(<https://manual.23people.io/about-us/>)

### Our Impact

23people has nearly a decade of existence, with more than 500 professionals having been part of its history and a current team of approximately 90 active professionals, primarily in technical roles such as Software Engineers, Data Engineers, Cloud Engineers, and AI Engineers. The company has served more than 10 large organizations across 3 continents — Latin America, North America, and Europe — executing close to 100 projects of varying complexity. This sustained growth is reflected in an average annual growth rate exceeding 30%.

The company's long-term goals establish three horizons: (1) **Driving Professional IT Growth** — ensuring that working at 23people represents a before-and-after milestone in each person's technical career; (2) **Becoming an Industry Reference** — being recognized by the market as a company others observe and seek to emulate, establishing "the 23people way" as a recognized standard; and (3) **Sustainable and Global Economic Growth** — multiplying current economic value tenfold at a pace the team can sustain indefinitely, with active presence in Latin America and Europe.

The company participates in the Chilean tech industry as a member of the **MITI Guild** (since 2022, a trade association of Chilean technology companies) and as a **Club CIO Sponsor** (since 2024, an invitation-only forum for IT directors of major Chilean companies).

(<https://manual.23people.io/about-us/social-proofs/>)

## What We Do

23people offers four specialized technology services designed to address the core challenges of enterprise digital evolution.

**1. 23people Tech Talent** — 23people forms, selects, and manages short- and long-term Tech Teams so clients can focus on their business objectives. The service evolves beyond traditional staff augmentation toward a full delegation model where the client entrusts 23people with forming and managing its technical capabilities. Two engagement approaches exist: a **Long-Term Approach** (for stable teams of 6+ months) delivered through Outsourcing Tech (subcontracting under 23people's legal structure) or Hunting Tech (search and selection for direct client hire); and a **Short-Term / High Specialization Approach** using 23people's own internal Engineering team for projects under 6 months. Differentiators include low voluntary rotation (below 20% annually), average professional tenure over 1.6 years per project, favorable timezone alignment from Chile/LATAM for US clients, and high NPS scores from both professionals and clients.

**2. 23people Integrations** — 23people connects the systems organizations use most by designing, developing, and maintaining integrations that link systems via APIs and events, automate cross-system manual workflows, enable agility through satellite applications when core enterprise systems are rigid, and prepare infrastructure for modern use cases (analytics, AI, automation). The service covers financial and scoring system integrations, satellite applications for ERP environments (SAP, Oracle), cross-system workflow automation, and data pipelines for analytics and AI. Key industries served include financial services/fintech, healthcare, companies with complex ERPs, and retail/e-commerce. The technology stack is selected per project considering RabbitMQ, Apache Kafka, AWS SQS/SNS, n8n, Temporal.io, Node.js/TypeScript, Python, Go, Apache Airflow, dbt, Docker/Kubernetes, and Terraform.

**3. 23people Cloud Optimization** — 23people advises and implements improvements to minimize cloud infrastructure costs for client platforms without sacrificing performance or security, including migrations, modernizations, and hybrid cloud + on-premise architectures using secure connectors and API Gateways.

**4. 23people Data & AI** — 23people implements the engineering to generate, collect, and structure the data organizations need for decision-making and AI-based solutions. This includes data pipelines for analytics and machine learning (extracting from CRM, ERP, transactional platforms, loading into data warehouses like Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift), and document integration pipelines for RAG systems and AI agents (embeddings, vector search, LangChain, LlamaIndex).

The client challenges 23people addresses are: (1) justification of return on investment in digital evolution — technology and data alone do not generate value, tangible improvements in business processes are required; (2) need for a reliable technology partner that understands both technology and business objectives; (3) difficulty in identifying and retaining genuinely skilled and passionate tech professionals in a competitive market.

Notable client success cases include:

- **Equifax USA / Santiago Development Center**: 23people formed the first development team in 2 weeks and scaled to managing over 50 professionals across 6 simultaneous projects, with 13 professionals later hired directly by Equifax. Projects supported the InterConnect credit decision platform.
- **ACHS (COVID-19 Telemedicine)**: 23people developed an integral telemedicine and appointment scheduling platform during the pandemic, meeting regulatory requirements and integrating with SAP consultants, IMED, and payment systems.
- **Equifax Chile**: Modernization of the Platinum 360 report platform.
- **FALP**: Integration with Equifax for credit scoring.

(<https://manual.23people.io/what-we-do/>)

## How We Do It

23people's operational approach rests on three main pillars.

**Pillar 1 — Tech Role Specialization**: 23people specializes exclusively in recruiting exceptional Software Engineers and programmers — professionals who are genuinely passionate about what they do and whose declared experience can be verified. The company works with four primary role categories: **Software Engineers** (Fullstack, Frontend, Backend, QA), **Data Engineers**, **Cloud Engineers** (DevOps, SRE, Cloud Architects), and **AI Engineers** (Data Scientists, ML Engineers, Deep Learning Engineers). Three seniority levels are used — Entry, Intermediate, and Senior — defined by skill development and demonstrated responsibility rather than years of experience. Once exceptional professionals are identified, 23people focuses on retaining them through genuine technical growth, continuous learning challenges, and a culture where passion for technology drives contribution.

**Pillar 2 — Enterprise Software Engineering Approach**: 23people co-builds enterprise software in an agile, evolutionary, and data-driven manner. The development methodology is Kanban-based, prioritizing continuous flow, WIP limits, and iterative improvement. Software development principles include simplicity as the primary driver, a balance between Object-Oriented Programming and Functional Programming, SOLID principles, business-centered thinking, scalability, and transparency through collaboration. The core technology stack centers on Node.js/NestJS, React, Python (for ML), MongoDB, and PostgreSQL, complemented by an extended ecosystem covering cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP), AI tools (OpenAI, Anthropic, LlamaIndex, RAG architectures), containerization (Docker/Kubernetes), and CI/CD with GitHub Actions. Development practices include Git-based version control workflows, mandatory code reviews, automated testing at multiple levels, CI/CD pipelines, pair/mob programming, monitoring and observability, and infrastructure as code.

**Pillar 3 — Customer Experience Excellence**: 23people pursues exceptional satisfaction in every client interaction. Clients must feel heard, advised, and supported at each step of the engagement. The service delivery model involves a core Squad (Service Delivery Manager, Tech Lead, and development team) supported by consulting specialists (UX Designer, Quality Assurance, Architect) who interface with the client's Product Owner to deliver increments.

(<https://manual.23people.io/how-we-do/>)

## Organization

### Overview

23people organizes itself in multidisciplinary teams, each with a clearly defined purpose and responsibility for delivering services to internal teams or external clients. The organizational structure includes two layers: **Global Direction** (Goals, Strategy, Initiatives) and **Global Operations** (Operations & Delivery, Information Security & Compliance, Data Governance), which oversees the operational teams.

KPIs at 23people measure observable behaviors — not opinions or surveys. They are organized around four dimensions: **Growth** (revenue, pipeline, capacity), **Diversification** (balanced growth across clients and services), **Quality** (maintaining standards while growing), and **Efficiency** (operational and financial improvement). Global KPIs include Net Revenue vs. Planned, Revenue Concentration by Client, Revenue Distribution by Service Line, Voluntary Professional Turnover Rate, and Client Retention Rate.

(<https://manual.23people.io/teams/>)

### Tribes and Teams

23people uses the concept of **Tribe** to group teams sharing common objectives and purposes.

**Tribu Iarvis** groups all operational teams of 23people. Its purpose is to enhance the delivery of high-quality, sustainable services aligned with 23people's strategic objectives through effective collaboration among its operational teams. The Iarvis Tribe comprises four operational teams: Tech Talent Services (TTS), Sales & Marketing (SMKT), Backoffice (BKO), and Engineering (ENG).

**Tribu Drakkar** groups all teams and professionals working in subcontracting mode — either as part of a dedicated team or as independent contractors. This tribe is essential for service delivery to clients, ensuring 23people has the right talent to meet client needs. Drakkar Tribe teams are managed jointly by TTS and BKO.

(<https://manual.23people.io/about-us/organization/>)

### Councils

Global Operations serves as the governing layer responsible for Operations & Delivery, Information Security & Compliance, and Data Governance. This council provides strategic oversight and ensures organizational alignment across all operational teams and client-facing activities. Global Direction defines the company's Goals, Strategy, and Initiatives, providing the top-level mandate that cascades to all operational teams through Global Operations.

(<https://manual.23people.io/about-us/organization/>)

### Workforce Structure

23people's workforce is primarily composed of tech professionals (Software Engineers, Data Engineers, Cloud Engineers, AI Engineers) organized into client-dedicated teams within the Drakkar Tribe, supported by the operational backbone of the Iarvis Tribe. The Backoffice team manages people growth processes including onboarding, offboarding, contracts, compensation, benefits, certifications, and labor compliance. TTS is responsible for recruitment, validation, and retention of tech professionals. The organization defines roles using three seniority levels (Entry, Intermediate, Senior) based on demonstrated skills rather than years of experience.

Each operational team tracks specific KPIs: **TTS** monitors vacancy completion rate, candidate acceptance rate by client, candidate lead time compliance, total new hires, and referral application rate. **Sales & Marketing** tracks qualified prospects (pipeline), digital media presence, net sales (UF), sales distribution by service line, client retention rate, and average sales cycle. **Backoffice** measures voluntary turnover, average tenure, professional engagement, labor compliance, timely billing rate, effective collection rate, tax compliance, net revenue (UF), EBITDA margin, gross margin, and revenue concentration. **Engineering** tracks engineering service revenue, features deployed to production, user-reported defects, average delivery lead time, and project renewal rate.

(<https://manual.23people.io/teams/>)

## Principles

23people's principles serve as objective behavioral guidelines and decision-making frameworks — not aspirations, but shared standards of the current organization.

**Fundamental Principles** define the organizational essence:

- **Excellence and Intellectual Curiosity**: Pursue excellence and continuous learning; apply iterative, flexible, data-based working methods; maintain an open mindset as perpetual learners.
- **Collaboration and Assertive Communication**: Support each other genuinely under honest transparency; co-create with clients and colleagues; prioritize team responsibility over individual heroism; prefer open communication channels over private ones.
- **Sustainable Growth and Becoming a Reference**: Pursue ambitious economic growth at a systematic, sustainable pace; avoid growth at the expense of people or natural resources; prefer systematic advances over exhausting one-time efforts.

**Culture and People Principles** define relational standards:

- **People First**: The company exists to serve human beings, not the other way around. Prioritize mutual benefit between the company and workers; recognize leaders over bosses; commit to being part of the personal success of clients and collaborators.
- **Equity**: Treat all people with respect and equality; avoid discrimination based on nationality, university, school, or location; apply positive discrimination when two equally evaluated candidates differ in representation of underrepresented groups.
- **Xperience**: Create meaningful, memorable experiences for every person who interacts with 23people — clients, tech professionals, administrative staff, and suppliers — recognizing that the quality of each interaction is fundamental to generating lasting value.

**Operational Principles** guide how the organization works:

- **Agility**: Apply agile values (individuals over processes, working software over documentation, client collaboration over contract negotiation, response to change over following a plan); enable rapid adaptation, continuous improvement cycles, and resource efficiency.
- **Transparency**: Make public everything that can be made public; default to open information except for client, employee, and supplier data or legally private information; recognize that transparency benefits — productivity, retention, recruitment, brand recognition — outweigh its costs.
- **Sustainability**: Commit to long-term vision for correct use of natural, financial, and social resources; prioritize remote work and 100% cloud/digital operations; use cloud services and data centers with clean energy; measure carbon footprint and promote recycling practices.

(<https://manual.23people.io/principles/>)

## Community

23people builds a community centered on the wellbeing, professional development, and connection of all its professionals. The community section consolidates benefits, events, administrative procedures, and shared spaces available to all 23people team members with at least 3 months of tenure (with some exceptions noted per benefit).

**Agreements (Convenios)**:

- **Betterfly**: A wellness and life insurance app that rewards healthy behaviors with BetterCoins. Provides life insurance through Vida Security (base + dynamic coverage), unlimited free telemedicine (medicine, psychology, nutrition), access to fitness and mindfulness platforms, and legal and financial advisory services.
- **Banco de Chile**: Banking account agreement for direct salary deposits.
- **Seguros Complementarios**: Supplementary health and dental insurance (conditions vary).

**Incentives**:

- **Vacation Incentive**: A cash bonus provided for each approved week of vacation taken (up to 3 weeks per year), reinforcing the importance of rest and disconnection.
- **Birthday Gift and Free Day**: A gift and a free day off on the employee's birthday.
- **National Holidays Bonus (Fiestas Patrias y Navidad)**: Additional bonuses for Chilean national holidays and Christmas.
- **Baby Shower**: A gift benefit for employees expecting a child.

**Reimbursements**:

- **Influenza Vaccination**: Reimbursement for annual flu vaccine.
- **Online Courses**: Annual reimbursement budget (CLP $120,000 per year, in quarterly tranches) for professional development courses on platforms such as Udemy, Coursera, and Platzi.
- **International Certifications**: Reimbursement support (up to 200 USD) for internationally recognized certifications in technology, project management methodologies, and language proficiency (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure, CISSP, Scrum, Cambridge, IELTS).
- **Workspace Improvement**: Reimbursement for home office improvement expenses.

**Training**:

- **English Classes**: Group English classes (max 5 people per group) from A2 to C1 levels, running approximately 3 months per cycle, opening every 4 months.

**Administrative Procedures**: Vacation requests, reimbursements, and related administrative processes are handled via established internal channels, documented in the community procedures section.

(<https://manual.23people.io/community/>)

## Technical Guide

23people's technical guide establishes guidelines and best practices for software development, rooted in the principle that building software is fundamentally about understanding and simplifying real problems.

**Development Principles**: (1) Simplicity above all — eliminate unnecessary complexity at every layer from architecture to code; (2) Balance between OOP and Functional Programming — applying encapsulation, abstraction, inheritance, polymorphism alongside pure functions, immutability, and side-effect minimization; (3) SOLID Principles — Single Responsibility, Open/Closed, Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation, Dependency Inversion; (4) Business-centered focus — understand the problem before writing code, deliver value to clients and end users; (5) Scalability and maintainability — design for controlled, orderly growth; (6) Transparency and collaboration — open feedback loops between technical and non-technical team members.

**Methodology**: A Kanban-based agile approach with workflow visualization on Kanban boards, WIP limits to prevent overload, periodic reviews for continuous improvement, and adaptability to combine with other techniques (sprints, retrospectives) when projects require it. The methodology aligns with 23people's simplification culture: lightweight, adaptable, focused on delivering value in small, manageable units.

**Core Technology Stack**: Node.js/NestJS (scalable server-side applications with modular architecture), React (interactive user interfaces), Python (machine learning and data science with NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch), MongoDB (flexible document storage for rapidly evolving data models), and PostgreSQL (robust relational database for ACID-compliant, structured data requirements).

**Development Practices**: Git-based version control (GitFlow or GitHub Flow), mandatory code reviews for all changes, automated testing (unit, integration, end-to-end), CI/CD pipelines for automated build/test/deploy cycles, pair and mob programming for complex problems, production monitoring and observability (logging, dashboards), concise and up-to-date documentation, and containerization with Docker and infrastructure as code (IaC) for environment reproducibility.

**AI and Machine Learning**: 23people works with RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) architectures, AI agents, Transformers, fine-tuning, prompt engineering, and guardrails. Supported LLM providers include Anthropic (Claude Models, Claude Code), OpenAI (GPT Models), Deepseek, and Ollama for local deployments. Frameworks include LlamaIndex, LangChain (via integrations), Hugging Face, TensorFlow, and PyTorch.

**Cloud and Infrastructure**: The company operates across AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and Cloudflare. Infrastructure practices include Docker/Kubernetes for cloud-native deployments, Terraform for IaC, serverless architectures (AWS Lambda, Cloud Functions) for event-driven workloads, and selection of cloud providers with renewable energy certifications aligned with sustainability commitments.

(<https://manual.23people.io/technical-guide/>)

## Sustainability

23people is committed to reducing its carbon footprint in alignment with climate science and the Paris Agreement objectives. As a 100% digitized and remote company, it has adopted Science Based Targets (SBTi) to ensure growth does not compromise climate balance.

**Emission Reduction Objectives**: Reduce at least 50% of Scope 1 and 2 emissions by 2030 (vs. baseline), achieve carbon neutrality by 2040, optimize the environmental impact of digital infrastructure by migrating to cloud servers with renewable energy certifications, and reduce Scope 3 emissions (business travel, personal devices, outsourced services) by 30%.

**Implementation Strategy**: The Carbon Neutral certification roadmap includes four phases — initial measurement using GHG Protocol methodology with local certifier partnerships, implementation of reduction strategies (Efficient Homes program for remote workers, Efficient Digitalization program with green coding and low-carbon cloud selection, Responsible Mobility program), compensation through certified reforestation and renewable energy carbon offset projects in Chile, and formal Carbon Neutral certification.

**Governance**: A Sustainability Committee with representatives from each operational team reports directly to general management and meets monthly. Roles include a Sustainability Leader for overall coordination, Green Ambassadors in each team, and a Tech Sustainability Lead focused on digital infrastructure efficiency. Carbon footprint is measured annually with specialized tools, reported transparently in sustainability reports, and submitted to SBTi for official validation.

**Operational Sustainability Practices**: 23people defines itself as a 100% digital and 100% cloud company, which optimizes resource use and reduces environmental impact. Remote work is prioritized for its social and environmental benefits. Cloud services with clean energy data centers are preferred. Recycling and reuse practices are promoted across the organization.

(<https://manual.23people.io/sustainability/>)

---

## How to Use This Summary (For AI Tools)

This document provides a stable, factual summary of the 23people Company Handbook optimized for LLM consumption. Use it to:

- **Answer questions about 23people's identity, mission, and values**: Refer to the "About Us" section for founding history, mission statement, core values, impact metrics, and industry partnerships.
- **Explain 23people's services and value proposition**: The "What We Do" section covers all four service lines (Tech Talent, Integrations, Cloud Optimization, Data & AI), client challenges addressed, and notable success cases.
- **Describe how 23people works**: The "How We Do It" section covers the three operational pillars — tech role specialization, software engineering approach, and customer experience focus.
- **Explain organizational structure**: The "Organization" section covers the Iarvis and Drakkar tribe structure, the four operational teams (TTS, SMKT, BKO, ENG), KPI framework, and workforce composition.
- **Reference principles for decision guidance**: The "Principles" section covers all nine principles organized by category (Fundamental, Culture & People, Operational) with application guidance.
- **Describe employee benefits and community**: The "Community" section covers agreements, incentives, reimbursements, and training programs.
- **Explain technical standards**: The "Technical Guide" section covers development principles, Kanban methodology, core tech stack, and engineering practices.
- **Address sustainability topics**: The "Sustainability" section covers SBTi commitments, emission reduction targets, the Carbon Neutral roadmap, and governance structure.

For more detailed information on any topic, refer to the full handbook at [https://manual.23people.io](https://manual.23people.io). Every section URL is provided inline above for direct reference.

**Important**: All content in this summary is factual and derived exclusively from source handbook documents. Do not invent or extrapolate specific KPI values, individual role assignments, current project details, or client-specific confidential information. When in doubt, direct users to the full handbook for the most current information.
